title: 3.23 Commuter Day Farmers Market
description:
Celebrate Commuter Day at the Dolphin Farmers Market! ????
Join us on March 23 from 12:00–2:00 pm for an afternoon of food, fun, and community. Enjoy a lively cheer performance, explore a variety of food trucks and local vendors, and get creative with DIY keychains and air fresheners. Stop by the photobooth to capture the moment, challenge friends to lawn games, and connect with helpful campus resources.
Whether you’re stopping by between classes or spending the whole afternoon with friends, there’s something for everyone, come out and enjoy the festivities!

title: Modoc Harvest Days
description:
Join Basic Needs for a hands-on Garden Harvest Day at Modoc Garden! Help harvest fresh seasonal produce that will be distributed through Basic Needs Dolphin Pantry and take some home for yourself.

No gardening experience needed—just bring comfy clothes, a hat, wear sunscreen, and closed-toe shoes. Come learn simple harvesting skills, connect with fellow Dolphins, and support food access on campus.

Let’s grow and gather together to nourish our CSUCI community! ??

title: Wellness Workshop: Physical Health
description:
Strong body, sharper mind. This interactive workshop brings nutrition and fitness together for a practical, real-life look at how to feel your best. We’ll review the science of exercise, different types of physical activity, and how getting proper nutrition plays a role in your health and wellness goals.
Plus, we’ll highlight campus resources that make staying active and well-fed easier than you think.
Click here to RSVP!

Students who complete all workshops will receive an Applied Learning & Career Readiness Certificate.

title: S.A.F.E. Training
description:
Students, Administrators, and Faculty for Equality (SAFE) workshop facilitated by Edgar Euan.

Edgar Euan is an advocate and speaker with over a decade of experience helping communities, companies, and nonprofits create spaces where everyone belongs. Known for making big ideas practical, he brings stories, data, and real talk to every stage—whether it’s a keynote, workshop, or training. Edgar’s work is fueled by authenticity and the belief that true inclusion isn’t just policy—it’s culture, power, and belonging in action.

title: Wellness Workshop: Sexual Health
description:
Let’s talk about it — clearly, confidently, and without awkward myths. This evidence-based workshop focuses on preventing sexually transmitted infections (STIs) through safer sex practices, including barrier methods and contraceptive options. We’ll also cover anatomy basics, consent, and how to make informed decisions that protect both you and your partners.
You’ll leave with accurate information, practical strategies, and awareness of campus resources that support your sexual health. Knowledge is power — and prevention is care.
Click here to RSVP!

title: Echoes of Land Album Release Celebration
description:
Echoes of Land

Album Release Celebration

Please come celebrate with us Dr. KuanFen Liu’s debut album release, Echoes of Land Thursday, April 16 from 3-5 PM on the CSU Channel Island’s Campus in Malibu Hall 100. Please celebrate with a live performance featuring a brief discussion and presentation with Dr. Liu and composers: Bevan Manson and Ashley Hoyer along with a live performance featuring 

Ashley Hoyer on mandolin, Evan Taucher on guitar, Bevan Manson on the piano plus a string quartet: Sharon Cooper and Cyndi Hall on the violin, Judy Garf on viola, and John Fare on cello..

Light refreshments will be served. Seating is limited, please RSVP HERE. 

Listen to a preview of the  album here. 

 

Bevan Manson composer
Ashley Hoyer composer

Channel Islands Chamber Orchestra
KuanFen Liu conductor

CSU Channel Island’s Release Celebration April 16, 2026

3-5 PM Malibu Hall 100

 

ECHOES OF THE LAND introduces the Channel Islands Chamber Orchestra in works by composers Ashley Hoyer and Bevan Manson, each exploring the character and history of distinct landscapes. From the Santa Monica Mountains, with their rare ecosystems and deep cultural roots, to the diverse environments of the Channel Islands and their centuries of human and natural stories, the album traces a musical voyage shaped by place. It closes in Odesa, where transplanted American trees became integral to the city’s identity. Together, these works illuminate the enduring connections between land, legacy, and the environments we inhabit.

 

ECHOES OF THE LAND are now available on Amazon Music, Apple Music, Spotify, DEEZER (French music streaming service), and TIDAL (available in 61 countries).

title: *Admitted Dolphin Day | North Quad
description:
Admitted Dolphin Day - April 18, 2026


Welcome to Admitted Dolphin Day at Cal State Channel Islands! ??We’re excited to invite you and your family/supporters to a special day just for you on Saturday, April 18, 2026. 
This exclusive event is for incoming freshmen and transfer students who have been provisionally admitted to CSUCI for Fall 2026. We can’t wait to welcome you all to the Dolphin family! ??

Register today!!

Please note, to register admitted students will need to know your myCI login information. For questions or assistance, please email admissions@csuci.edu or call at 805-437-8520. 



Secure Your Spot!


Let us know you'll be joining CSUCI in the Fall by confirming your enrollment. Secure your spot by May 1, 2026.





Unable to Attend?


If you are unable to attend our event, we encourage you to visit us by taking a campus tour at another time. Visit go.csuci.edu/visit to schedule a tour. For upcoming dates and deadlines for Fall 2026, visit our Admitted Student webpage. If you need further assistance, please contact Admissions at 805-437-8520 or Admissions@csuci.edu.


Accessibility Accommodations


The University encourages people with disabilities to participate in its programs, events and activities. If you anticipate needing any type of accommodation or have questions about the physical access provided, please contact the respective area noted below as soon as possible, but no later than seven (7) business days prior to the event/activity:

CSUCI Students: accommodations@csuci.edu
CSUCI Employees and Members of the Public: angela.portillo@csuci.edu

title: SPB Together We Craft-Senior Special
description: Celebrate the end of the semester in style with Together We Craft: Senior Special! Join SPB on May 5 from 3–5 PM in Coville for a fun, creative bedazzle afternoon. Seniors—bring your graduation cap and make it sparkle for commencement! All other students are welcome too—bring any item you’d like to bedazzle and give it a personal glow-up. We’ll provide all the supplies, so come hang out, get creative, and add some shine to your celebration.
location: Student Union Building 1080 - Conference Room, Student Union Game Lounge

title: Wellness Workshop: Sleep Hygiene
description:
In this workshop, you’ll learn how sleep cycles work, how rest impacts memory and academic success, and what’s really happening when you “power through” on five hours. We’ll cover common sleep disruptors, sleep disorders, the truth about naps (yes, there’s a right way to nap), and practical tips to help you build a sustainable sleep routine.
Because being exhausted shouldn’t be your personality trait. We’ll also share campus resources that support healthy sleep habits.
Click here to RSVP!

title: ASI Dolphin Drop-In
description:
Join us for Dolphin Drop-In, an interactive event where you can share your thoughts, enjoy free food, participate in fun activities, and win fun items! Engage with different stations, answer questions, and take part in exciting challenges. Complete the event survey for a chance to win giveaways.
Relax with lawn games, music, and more while connecting with fellow students. Don’t miss out on this opportunity to have fun and make your voice heard!
location: Student Union Dolphin Deck, Student Union Seabreeze Lawn
